topics.topic: INTRODUCTION
Importance of Machine Drawing and Production Drawing

Understand the concepts of Orthographic projections and Sectional views; Differentiate between machine drawing and production drawing; Explain abbreviations for materials, draughting abbreviations; State the factors that govern the preparation of a production drawing; Identify the components of a production drawing; Systems of fits-problems relating Hole basis and Shaft basis system and schematic diagrams- Select dimensions from B.I.S; Tables to obtain clearance, transition, and interference fit for a given set of mating parts; Selection of fits and tolerances from B.I.S. tables; Indicate fits on the drawings; Introduction and indication of form and position tolerances on drawings; INTRODUCTION Drawing Sheet Sizes, drawing sheet layout, Titl e block, Stan...
FASTENING DEVICES
Draw the standard thread profiles
Draw bolted connections to standard proportions
Draw different types of keys and cotters and Riveted Joints

FASTENING DEVICES Temporary and Permanent fastenings and their areas of application-thread nomenclature, forms of screw thread profiles, Bolts and Nuts: Specification of bolts and nuts, Different types of bolted joints (like usin g through bolts, studs, screws, eyebolt etc.,) in different applications. Purpose of lock nuts and their Types, Keys and cot ters: Types of keys and cotters. Riveted Joints- types
Practice and training on FASTENING DEVICES
Practice and training on Draw the standard thread profiles
Revision
Assessment / Practice / Problem Solving

units.title: Welding and Piping Layouts and Joints

topics.topic: WELDING AND PIPING LAYOUTS AND JOINTS
Identify the common components of a piping layout

Identify the conventional symbols used for the various components of piping layout; Prepare single-line and double-line diagrams of piping layouts; Identify the different types of welds and their symbolic representation as per B.I.S., SP- 46 2003; Identify the elements of the welding symbol and their standard location on the symbol; Explain Symbols indicating surface texture; Surface roughness obtainable from various manufacturing processes, recommended surface roughness on mechanical components; Symbols representing direction of lay; Heat treatment and surface treatment symbols used on drawings; WELDING AND PIPING LAYOUTS AND JOINTS Pipes and tubes. Classification-Components of pipe layout. Screw fitting Unions: Screwed ground and flanged, Valves: Gate valve, angle valve, check valve, Various conventional symbols used for the above components. Different types of welded Joints - basic symbols including sectional representation as per table of I.S. standards - Elements of welding symbol and their standard location, the symbol as per IS standards reference Code, arrow head, weld symbol, supplementary symbol, dimensions of welds, method of welding process, special reference. Supplementary symbols
Limits, Fits & Tolerances 3
Geometrical Tolerances 3

Surface finish 3; FITS, TOLERANCES &ENGINEERING MATERIALS; Limits, Fits & Tolerances; Importance of geometrical tolerances & indicating geometrical tolerances on the drawing; Surface finish; Specification of materials and Standard Components; Identify the materials of various components; Specify the Raw materials as per Commercial/ BIS Standards; Identify the standard part that can be procured directly from the market and specify the part as per Commercial/ BIS Standards; FITS, TOLERANCES & ENGINEERING MATERIALS Limits, allowance and fits -definitions- Select dimension from standards to give different type of fit for a given mating parts, Types of fits - problems relating Hole basis and Shaft basis system and schematic diagrams - Select dimensions from B. I. S. Tables to obtain clearance, transition and interference fit for a given set of mating parts, Select ion of fits and tolerances from B. I. S. tables, Indicate fits on the drawings. Indication of geometrical tolerance on the drawing, indication of feature controlled, form and position tolerances on drawings, Surface roughness terminology- surface roughness values, surface roughness obtainable from various manufacturing processes, recommended surface roughness on mechanical components. Materials of the parts of the assembly -size of the part, estimation of the Raw material required for a component and specification. Standard components: Parts like bolts, nuts, and Bearings, etc

units.title: PART-B Assembly and Detailed Part Drawing

topics.topic: ASSEMBLY AND DETAILED PART DRAWING
Indicate the sequence of the production
List the sequence of steps for preparing the assembly drawing
Prepare the list of parts
Prepare the assembly drawing for the given components drawing
Prepare the relevant views of the parts of the assembly drawing

ASSEMBLY AND DETAILED PART DRAWING Need and functions of assembly and detailed drawings. Steps in preparing assembly drawings. Bill of materials and parts list. Exercises in preparing assembly drawings of commonly available engineering components. Indicate the sequence of the production, specify the relevant tools to obtain the accuracy and finish. Indicate the suitable equipment, specify the type of measuring instruments to be used to check the prescribed accuracy, and specify of standard components. Prepare Assembly and detailed part drawings of the machine components of the following machine parts: Draw only the front view and top view (Full Section / Half Section with dimensions, including the Bill of materials, of the machine elements in the Drawing Sheet. Knuckle Joint Flanged Coupling Stuffing Box Cross head Eccentric Foot Step bearing Plummer Block Screw Jack Lathe Tool post

Assessment / Practice / Problem Solving
CAD MODELLING

CAD MODELLING Parametric CAD software (in any one of the above -mentioned software) – sketch –entities: line – circle – arc – ellipse – polygon – text – dimensions –sketch tools – fillet – chamfer – offset – trim – extend – mirror – rotate – block. Part modelling– reference planes – reference point – reference axes – coordinate system. Parametric CAD software – extrude – revolve – sweep – helix and spiral – shell – draft – wrap – intersect – holes – patterns. Assembly – approaches – mate – coincident – sub assembly –rebuild – isolate. Drawing views–Save – Plot – model view – exploded view – projected view – section view – import– export – Appearance – rendering. COMPUTER AIDED DRAFTING (CAD) Detailed drawings of any five of the above machine parts shall be given to students to create both part drawings and assembly drawings in CAD software, generate solid models, and take printouts REFERENCE BOOK 1. Engineering Drawing by A.C. Parkinson. 2. Machine Drawing by N.D. Bhat. 3. Machine Drawing by R.B. Gupta. 4. Machine Drawing by N.Siddeswar, Kannaih, Sastri. (MGH Publishers) 5. IS 696 – 1972-Code of Practice for General Engg. Drawing & B.I.S Code– SP. 46. IS696– 1988-IS Code on fits and tolerances. 6. K.L. Narayana, P. Kannaiah, & K. Venkata Reddy “ A Textbook of Machine Drawing” 7. R.K. Jain, Engineering Metrology, 2016 Khanna Publications 8. Machine Drawing by G.R Nagpal 9. Production Drawing by K.L. Narayana, P. Kannaiah, & K. Venkata Reddy Assessment: Formative Assessment: 40 Marks 20 Marks: Based on the sheets submitted by the students. 20 Marks: Awarded after conducting unit tests on CAD modelling. Summative Assessment: 60 Marks Part A: Four questions from Part A of the curriculum (4 × 5 = 20 Marks). Part B: One question to be answered out of two options: (1 × 40 = 40 Marks). Option 1: Assembly Drawing Option 2: Part Drawing
